methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 38 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Kyutai is a growth-stage AI & Software company founded by Xavier Niel that has raised €300M to date. The valuation universe comprises a broad AI & Software sector with 38 total peers—1 public (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and 8 private firms including OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, Databricks, and others. However, the peer set lacks sufficient depth at Kyutai's specific stage and valuation tier for reliable comparable-company analysis.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. A search for same-stage and adjacent-stage peers yielded only 1 datapoint across relevant filters, falling below the minimum threshold of 3 required for meaningful triangulation. The broader AI & Software sector contains 38 public and private peers, but filtering for stage alignment and similar valuation tier produced insufficient granularity. Using an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise and misrepresent company-specific dynamics.

This analysis cannot be synthesized into a supported valuation range given the methodological constraints. The core uncertainty is the absence of a sufficiently dense peer cohort at Kyutai's growth stage with comparable capital deployment and market positioning. Additional private-market transactions or a clearer definition of Kyutai's sub-sector (foundation models vs. infrastructure vs. applications) would be required to apply meaningful comparables-based valuation. Alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or venture capital method would be necessary to establish a defensible range.
